---
title: "NYT"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-08"
---
```{r}
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EPL HOME WIN PREDICTION MODEL
# Dependent  : home_win (1 = home win, 0 = draw or away win)
# Independent: p_home (bookmaker fair probability) + xG rolling features (all 5 types)
#
# KEY CHANGES FROM PREVIOUS GOAL-PREDICTION CODE:
#  - One row per match (was: two rows per match)
#  - home_win is the outcome (was: goals count)
#  - p_home from bookmaker is a predictor, not just for comparison
#  - Logistic regression + XGBoost binary:logistic (was: Poisson/NB + count:poisson)
#  - Simple rolling mean (was: exponentially-weighted rolling mean)
#  - SD removed entirely (was: sd_xG / opp_sd_xG)
#  - No Poisson score-matrix conversion step needed
#  - Elo removed entirely
#  - All 5 xG situation types used (openplay, corner, freekick, penalty, setpiece)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── PACKAGES ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(worldfootballR)
library(tidyverse)
library(zoo)
library(xgboost)
library(purrr)

select <- dplyr::select
filter <- dplyr::filter

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1: LOAD & AGGREGATE SHOT-LEVEL xG DATA
# (same source as before — understat EPL shots)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

epl_shots <- load_understat_league_shots(league = "EPL")

match_xg <- epl_shots %>%
  filter(season >= 2016, season <= 2024) %>%
  mutate(h_a = coalesce(h_a, home_away)) %>%
  group_by(match_id, league, season, date, home_team, away_team,
           home_goals, away_goals) %>%
  summarise(
    home_xG_openplay = sum(xG[h_a == "h" & situation == "OpenPlay"],       na.rm = TRUE),
    away_xG_openplay = sum(xG[h_a == "a" & situation == "OpenPlay"],       na.rm = TRUE),
    home_xG_corner   = sum(xG[h_a == "h" & situation == "FromCorner"],     na.rm = TRUE),
    away_xG_corner   = sum(xG[h_a == "a" & situation == "FromCorner"],     na.rm = TRUE),
    home_xG_freekick = sum(xG[h_a == "h" & situation == "DirectFreekick"], na.rm = TRUE),
    away_xG_freekick = sum(xG[h_a == "a" & situation == "DirectFreekick"], na.rm = TRUE),
    home_xG_penalty  = sum(xG[h_a == "h" & situation == "Penalty"],        na.rm = TRUE),
    away_xG_penalty  = sum(xG[h_a == "a" & situation == "Penalty"],        na.rm = TRUE),
    home_xG_setpiece = sum(xG[h_a == "h" & situation == "SetPiece"],       na.rm = TRUE),
    away_xG_setpiece = sum(xG[h_a == "a" & situation == "SetPiece"],       na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(date)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2: RESHAPE TO TEAM LEVEL
# Still needed to compute per-team rolling averages correctly.
# (We will collapse back to match-level in Step 4.)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_df <- bind_rows(
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season, date,
    team        = home_team,
    opponent    = away_team,
    home_away   = "home",
    xG_openplay = home_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = home_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = home_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = home_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = home_xG_setpiece
  ),
  match_xg %>% transmute(
    match_id, season, date,
    team        = away_team,
    opponent    = home_team,
    home_away   = "away",
    xG_openplay = away_xG_openplay,
    xG_corner   = away_xG_corner,
    xG_freekick = away_xG_freekick,
    xG_penalty  = away_xG_penalty,
    xG_setpiece = away_xG_setpiece
  )
) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  arrange(team, date)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 3: SIMPLE ROLLING MEANS (last 5 games, plain average)
#
# REMOVED vs previous code:
#  - exp_weights / exponentially-weighted mean (wmean5)
#  - sd_xG (standard deviation of total xG)
#
# Plain rollapply mean is used instead. The lag(1) ensures we
# only use information available BEFORE the match being predicted.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_df <- model_df %>%
  group_by(team) %>%
  mutate(
    roll_xG_openplay = lag(rollapply(xG_openplay, 5, mean, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_corner   = lag(rollapply(xG_corner,   5, mean, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_freekick = lag(rollapply(xG_freekick, 5, mean, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_penalty  = lag(rollapply(xG_penalty,  5, mean, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1),
    roll_xG_setpiece = lag(rollapply(xG_setpiece, 5, mean, fill = NA, align = "right"), 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 4: BUILD MATCH-LEVEL DATA FRAME
#
# NEW vs previous code:
#  - One row per match (not two rows per team)
#  - home_win is the dependent variable (1 = home win, 0 = draw/away win)
#  - Features are prefixed home_ / away_ instead of opp_
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

home_feats <- model_df %>%
  filter(home_away == "home") %>%
  select(
    match_id, season, date,
    home_team                = team,
    away_team                = opponent,
    home_roll_xG_openplay    = roll_xG_openplay,
    home_roll_xG_corner      = roll_xG_corner,
    home_roll_xG_freekick    = roll_xG_freekick,
    home_roll_xG_penalty     = roll_xG_penalty,
    home_roll_xG_setpiece    = roll_xG_setpiece
  )

away_feats <- model_df %>%
  filter(home_away == "away") %>%
  select(
    match_id,
    away_roll_xG_openplay    = roll_xG_openplay,
    away_roll_xG_corner      = roll_xG_corner,
    away_roll_xG_freekick    = roll_xG_freekick,
    away_roll_xG_penalty     = roll_xG_penalty,
    away_roll_xG_setpiece    = roll_xG_setpiece
  )

match_df <- match_xg %>%
  select(match_id, season, date, home_team, away_team,
         home_goals, away_goals) %>%
  mutate(
    date     = as.Date(date),
    home_win = as.integer(home_goals > away_goals)   # 1 = win, 0 = draw or loss
  ) %>%
  left_join(home_feats, by = c("match_id", "season", "date", "home_team", "away_team")) %>%
  left_join(away_feats, by = "match_id") %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  arrange(date)

cat("Match-level rows:", nrow(match_df), "\n")
cat("Home win rate:   ", round(mean(match_df$home_win), 3), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 5: LOAD BOOKMAKER DATA & COMPUTE p_home
#
# p_home is the bookmaker's FAIR probability of a home win
# (overround removed). This is now a PREDICTOR in your model,
# not just a comparison target.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

pl <- readRDS("pl_clean.rds")

team_map <- c(
  "Man City"      = "Manchester City",
  "Man United"    = "Manchester United",
  "Newcastle"     = "Newcastle United",
  "Nott'm Forest" = "Nottingham Forest",
  "West Brom"     = "West Bromwich Albion",
  "Wolves"        = "Wolverhampton Wanderers"
)

pl_fair <- pl %>%
  mutate(
    HomeTeam = recode(HomeTeam, !!!team_map),
    AwayTeam = recode(AwayTeam, !!!team_map),
    Date     = as.Date(Date),
    # Raw implied probabilities
    impl_home = 1 / B365H,
    impl_draw = 1 / B365D,
    impl_away = 1 / B365A,
    overround = impl_home + impl_draw + impl_away,
    # Fair (margin-removed) probabilities
    p_home = impl_home / overround,
    p_draw = impl_draw / overround,
    p_away = impl_away / overround
  ) %>%
  select(Date, HomeTeam, AwayTeam, p_home, p_draw, p_away)

# Join bookmaker data to match_df
match_df <- match_df %>%
  left_join(
    pl_fair,
    by = c("date" = "Date", "home_team" = "HomeTeam", "away_team" = "AwayTeam")
  )

cat("Rows with bookmaker data:", sum(!is.na(match_df$p_home)), "\n")
cat("Rows missing p_home:     ", sum(is.na(match_df$p_home)),  "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 6: FINAL CLEAN & TRAIN / TEST SPLIT
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

match_df <- match_df %>%
  drop_na(p_home,
          home_roll_xG_openplay, home_roll_xG_corner,
          home_roll_xG_freekick, home_roll_xG_penalty, home_roll_xG_setpiece,
          away_roll_xG_openplay, away_roll_xG_corner,
          away_roll_xG_freekick, away_roll_xG_penalty, away_roll_xG_setpiece)

train_df <- match_df %>% filter(season <= 2022)
test_df  <- match_df %>% filter(season >= 2023)

cat("Train rows:", nrow(train_df), "| Seasons:", min(train_df$season), "-", max(train_df$season), "\n")
cat("Test rows: ", nrow(test_df),  "| Seasons:", min(test_df$season),  "-", max(test_df$season),  "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 7: LOGISTIC REGRESSION MODELS
#
# Family = binomial(link = "logit")
# Dependent  : home_win (binary 0/1)
# Independent: p_home + xG rolling features (all 5 situation types)
#
# Why logistic and not Poisson/NB?
#  - home_win is binary → logistic is the correct GLM family
#  - Poisson/NB is for counts (goals); it cannot model 0/1 outcomes
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Model 1 — Bookmaker probability only (benchmark)
# Tests how well p_home alone predicts home wins
logit_book <- glm(
  home_win ~ p_home,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_book)

# Model 2 — Bookmaker + xG open-play only
logit_xg <- glm(
  home_win ~
    p_home +
    home_roll_xG_openplay +
    away_roll_xG_openplay,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_xg)

# Model 3 — Bookmaker + all 5 xG types (home and away)
logit_full <- glm(
  home_win ~
    p_home +
    home_roll_xG_openplay +
    home_roll_xG_corner   +
    home_roll_xG_freekick +
    home_roll_xG_penalty  +
    home_roll_xG_setpiece +
    away_roll_xG_openplay +
    away_roll_xG_corner   +
    away_roll_xG_freekick +
    away_roll_xG_penalty  +
    away_roll_xG_setpiece,
  data   = train_df,
  family = binomial(link = "logit")
)
summary(logit_full)

# AIC comparison
cat("\n=== AIC Comparison ===\n")
cat("Logit book only:  ", AIC(logit_book), "\n")
cat("Logit + xG:       ", AIC(logit_xg),   "\n")
cat("Logit full:       ", AIC(logit_full),  "\n")

# Generate predicted probabilities on test set
test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    pred_logit_book = predict(logit_book, newdata = test_df, type = "response"),
    pred_logit_xg   = predict(logit_xg,   newdata = test_df, type = "response"),
    pred_logit_full = predict(logit_full,  newdata = test_df, type = "response")
  )

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 8: XGBOOST — BINARY CLASSIFICATION
#
# Objective = "binary:logistic" (was: "count:poisson")
# Outputs a probability directly — no Poisson matrix conversion needed
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

feature_names <- c(
  "p_home",
  "home_roll_xG_openplay", "home_roll_xG_corner",
  "home_roll_xG_freekick", "home_roll_xG_penalty", "home_roll_xG_setpiece",
  "away_roll_xG_openplay", "away_roll_xG_corner",
  "away_roll_xG_freekick", "away_roll_xG_penalty", "away_roll_xG_setpiece"
)

train_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = as.matrix(train_df[, feature_names]),
  label = train_df$home_win           # binary 0/1
)

test_matrix <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = as.matrix(test_df[, feature_names]),
  label = test_df$home_win
)

xgb_model <- xgb.train(
  params = list(
    objective        = "binary:logistic",   # KEY CHANGE from count:poisson
    eval_metric      = "logloss",
    eta              = 0.05,
    max_depth        = 4,
    subsample        = 0.8,
    colsample_bytree = 0.8
  ),
  data                  = train_matrix,
  nrounds               = 500,
  evals                 = list(train = train_matrix, test = test_matrix),
  early_stopping_rounds = 30,
  verbose               = 1,
  print_every_n         = 50
)

test_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(pred_xgb = predict(xgb_model, test_matrix))

# Feature importance
importance <- xgb.importance(feature_names = feature_names, model = xgb_model)
xgb.plot.importance(importance)
print(importance)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 9: EVALUATE — LOG LOSS & BRIER SCORE
#
# Correct metrics for binary probability predictions:
#  - Log loss (lower = better): penalises confident wrong predictions
#  - Brier score (lower = better): mean squared error of probabilities
#
# We compare each model against the bookmaker's p_home baseline
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

log_loss <- function(actual, predicted) {
  p <- pmax(pmin(predicted, 1 - 1e-15), 1e-15)
  -mean(actual * log(p) + (1 - actual) * log(1 - p))
}

brier_score <- function(actual, predicted) {
  mean((actual - predicted)^2)
}

eval_results <- test_df %>%
  summarise(
    # Log loss
    ll_bookmaker    = log_loss(home_win, p_home),
    ll_logit_book   = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_book),
    ll_logit_xg     = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_xg),
    ll_logit_full   = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_full),
    ll_xgb          = log_loss(home_win, pred_xgb),
    # Brier score
    bs_bookmaker    = brier_score(home_win, p_home),
    bs_logit_book   = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_book),
    bs_logit_xg     = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_xg),
    bs_logit_full   = brier_score(home_win, pred_logit_full),
    bs_xgb          = brier_score(home_win, pred_xgb)
  )

cat("\n=== Model Evaluation (test set) ===\n")
cat("                  Log Loss   Brier Score\n")
cat("Bookmaker (p_home):", round(eval_results$ll_bookmaker,  4),
    "  ", round(eval_results$bs_bookmaker,  4), "\n")
cat("Logit book only:   ", round(eval_results$ll_logit_book, 4),
    "  ", round(eval_results$bs_logit_book, 4), "\n")
cat("Logit + xG:        ", round(eval_results$ll_logit_xg,   4),
    "  ", round(eval_results$bs_logit_xg,   4), "\n")
cat("Logit full:        ", round(eval_results$ll_logit_full,  4),
    "  ", round(eval_results$bs_logit_full,  4), "\n")
cat("XGBoost:           ", round(eval_results$ll_xgb,         4),
    "  ", round(eval_results$bs_xgb,         4), "\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 10: CALIBRATION CHECK
# Does your model's predicted probability match the actual win rate?
# A well-calibrated model should have: predicted ~= actual
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

calibration_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    prob_bucket = cut(pred_logit_full,
                      breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                      include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  group_by(prob_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n              = n(),
    avg_predicted  = mean(pred_logit_full),
    actual_win_rate = mean(home_win),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Calibration (logit full model) ===\n")
print(calibration_df)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 11: EDGE ANALYSIS vs BOOKMAKER
# edge = your predicted p_home - bookmaker's fair p_home
# Positive edge → you think home team is MORE likely to win
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Use the best-performing model — adjust this if XGBoost is best
eval_df <- test_df %>%
  mutate(
    edge_logit = pred_logit_full - p_home,
    edge_xgb   = pred_xgb        - p_home
  )

edge_analysis <- eval_df %>%
  mutate(
    edge_bucket = cut(edge_logit,
                      breaks = c(-1, -0.2, -0.1, 0, 0.1, 0.2, 1),
                      labels = c("Strong against", "Mild against", "Neutral low",
                                 "Neutral high",   "Mild for",     "Strong for"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(edge_bucket) %>%
  summarise(
    n              = n(),
    avg_edge       = mean(edge_logit),
    actual_rate    = mean(home_win),
    avg_model_prob = mean(pred_logit_full),
    avg_fair_prob  = mean(p_home),
    log_loss_model = log_loss(home_win, pred_logit_full),
    log_loss_book  = log_loss(home_win, p_home),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\n=== Performance by edge size (logit full) ===\n")
print(edge_analysis)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 12: SIMULATED BETTING ROI
# Bet on home win only when your model shows positive edge
# Returns use fair odds (1/p_home) as a proxy for actual odds
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

betting_returns <- function(df, edge_threshold, pred_col = "pred_logit_full") {
  bets <- df %>%
    mutate(edge = .data[[pred_col]] - p_home) %>%
    filter(edge > edge_threshold) %>%
    mutate(
      decimal_odds = 1 / p_home,
      profit       = if_else(home_win == 1, decimal_odds - 1, -1)
    )

  if (nrow(bets) == 0) {
    return(tibble(threshold = edge_threshold, n_bets = 0,
                  total_profit = NA, roi = NA, win_rate = NA))
  }

  tibble(
    threshold    = edge_threshold,
    n_bets       = nrow(bets),
    total_profit = round(sum(bets$profit), 2),
    roi          = round(sum(bets$profit) / nrow(bets) * 100, 2),
    win_rate     = round(mean(bets$home_win) * 100, 2),
    avg_edge     = round(mean(bets$edge), 3)
  )
}

roi_logit <- map_dfr(c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30),
                     ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_logit_full"))

roi_xgb   <- map_dfr(c(0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.30),
                     ~betting_returns(eval_df, .x, "pred_xgb"))

cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — Logit full ===\n")
print(roi_logit)

cat("\n=== Simulated ROI — XGBoost ===\n")
print(roi_xgb)

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 13: STATISTICAL TEST — IS YOUR EDGE REAL?
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

model_losses <- eval_df %>%
  mutate(
    loss_model = -(home_win * log(pmax(pred_logit_full, 1e-15)) +
                   (1-home_win) * log(pmax(1-pred_logit_full, 1e-15))),
    loss_book  = -(home_win * log(pmax(p_home, 1e-15)) +
                   (1-home_win) * log(pmax(1-p_home, 1e-15))),
    diff       = loss_model - loss_book    # negative = model is better
  )

t_result <- t.test(model_losses$diff, mu = 0)

cat("\n=== Statistical test: model log loss vs bookmaker ===\n")
cat("Mean difference (model - bookmaker):", round(mean(model_losses$diff), 4), "\n")
cat("Negative = model is better\n")
cat("p-value:", round(t_result$p.value, 4), "\n")
cat("If p < 0.05, the difference is statistically significant\n")

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SAVE OUTPUTS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

saveRDS(match_df, "epl_match_df.rds")
saveRDS(test_df,  "epl_test_results.rds")

cat("\nDone. Files saved.\n")
```
